kpi_definitions.business_definition: The percentage of customer interactions resolved without requiring a follow-up contact within 5 business days. An interaction is considered resolved if no subsequent inbound contact occurs from the same customer regarding the same issue within the window.
kpi_definitions.numerator: Interactions where fcr_flg = 'Y' (no callback within 5 business days)
kpi_definitions.ai_instruction: FCR measures whether a customer's issue was resolved in a single interaction. A high FCR means customers do not need to call back. FCR is calculated per interaction, not per customer. Exclude outbound welcome calls from FCR calculation.
verified_answer_triggers: how often do customers call back

kpi_definitions.business_definition: Average customer satisfaction score collected via post-call IVR survey on a 1-5 scale (5 = very satisfied). Survey is offered to all inbound calls; response rate approximately 22%. Score is weighted by queue type for aggregate reporting.
kpi_definitions.numerator: Sum of csat_score values where csat_score IS NOT NULL
kpi_definitions.denominator: Count of interactions with a non-null csat_score
kpi_definitions.ai_instruction: CSAT is a post-call IVR score from 1 to 5. Only approximately 22% of customers complete the survey. Do not interpret a missing CSAT as a bad score — it means the customer did not respond. Average CSAT below 3.5 on a queue indicates a systemic issue, not individual agent performance.

kpi_definitions.business_definition: Percentage of expiring Protection Plan contracts that are successfully renewed within the renewal window (30 days before to 15 days after contract end date). Applies to HVAC_PLAN and WH_RENTAL_PLAN product codes. Excludes contracts cancelled mid-term.
kpi_definitions.numerator: PP contracts with renewal_status_code = 'renewed' in the renewal window
kpi_definitions.denominator: All PP contracts that reached expiry (renewed + lapsed + cancelled), excluding mid-term cancellations
kpi_definitions.ai_instruction: PP Renewal Rate measures how many customers renew their HVAC or water heater protection plans. This is the primary retention KPI for Enercare. A renewal can happen via inbound call (recorded in dw_inbnd_t), outbound retention call, online portal, or direct mail. When a customer calls the billing queue before their renewal date and does not renew, it often signals billing confusion. PP = Protection Plan throughout this system.

kpi_definitions.business_definition: Average duration in seconds of an agent's active engagement per interaction, including talk time, hold time, and after-call wrap-up time. Measured per queue type. Target varies by queue (billing: 420s, emergency: 300s, PP renewal: 480s).
kpi_definitions.numerator: Sum of (handle_time_sec + hold_time_sec) for all interactions
kpi_definitions.ai_instruction: AHT = talk time + hold time + wrap time. AHT targets differ by queue: billing=420s, emergency=300s, PP_renewal=480s. High AHT on the billing queue combined with low CSAT often indicates agents cannot resolve the issue in one call, driving up re-contact rates.

kpi_definitions.business_definition: Percentage of field service visits where the technician did not arrive within the committed service window. SLA windows: emergency = 4 hours, maintenance = scheduled date, repair = next business day. Measured per equipment type.
kpi_definitions.ai_instruction: SLA Breach Rate tracks whether field service arrived on time. SLA breaches on HVAC emergency visits directly drive inbound call volume to the emergency queue and correlate with low CSAT. When SLA_BRCH_RATE on HVAC > 10%, expect AHT to increase on the hvac_service queue within 48 hours.
